query: Moscow Oil Refinery Moskovsky NPZ Kapotnya drone attacks late June and early July 2026 - has it been targeted again after June 18 2026, current operational status, and frequency of Ukrainian drone strikes on Moscow refinery
answer:
Research complete. Here's the bottom line on the Moscow Oil Refinery (Moskovsky NPZ / Kapotnya):

**Has it been targeted again after June 18, 2026?** No — there are **no public reports of a new strike on the Kapotnya refinery after June 18, 2026** as of July 3. The two documented direct attacks were June 16 and June 18, 2026 (the June 18 raid being the largest attack on Moscow since 2022).

**Current operational status:** The refinery is **offline** and, per Reuters/industry sources, unlikely to resume production this year — potentially out until **early 2027** (minimum ~6 months). Two processing units were damaged, including the Euro+ installation (~98bn rubles). It normally supplies ~40% of Moscow's fuel and ~70% of the capital region's gasoline.

**Frequency of Ukrainian strikes on Russian refineries:** The campaign is highly active, but late-June/early-July strikes hit **other** refineries (Ufa, Kstovo/Nizhny Novgorod, Saratov, etc.), not Kapotnya — because MNPZ was already neutralized. By end of June, Ukraine had struck **8 of Russia's top 10 refineries**, taking ~one-third of national refining capacity offline and triggering a Russian summer fuel crisis (rationing, long queues, export restrictions).

One caveat: AskNews real-time feeds were quota-blocked, so this rests on web search and direct article fetches.
